findings.problem: The purse beat reaches the chapter's ugliest leverage point and then sanitizes it. Marcia touches the wallet and feels the shame, but Elena's interruption resolves the moment before Marcia has to accept or refuse the money herself, so the scene keeps the embarrassment while defusing the moral consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the beat beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:135 and peaking through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:151 so Marcia makes a decisive choice on the page before Elena restores order. Either let her remove the bill and face the fallout, or make her refuse Celia explicitly and own what that refusal costs; do not let the intervention at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:145 short-circuit her choice.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:135 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:147, a reader can point to one exact sentence where Marcia clearly accepts or refuses the money by action or speech before Elena reclaims the purse. If Elena's "No" still settles the beat before Marcia chooses, this finding remains open.

findings.problem: The narration repeatedly steps out of Marcia's immediate humiliation to explain the rich-poor meaning in thesis sentences. One or two such turns would sharpen class pressure; this many starts to make the chapter sound argued rather than suffered, which keeps it below Booker-level subtlety.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the explanatory beats around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:113, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:195 so at least half of those moments convey hierarchy through observed behavior, bodily reaction, or room dynamics instead of abstract sentences about what rich people, women with money, or neighborhoods like this mean.
findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:113, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:195, no more than two explanatory group-label sentences about class remain, and at least two of those beats make the class hierarchy legible through concrete action or sensory detail alone.

findings.problem: The chapter leans four times on the same contrastive-definition move: a thing is named by saying what it is not and then relabeling it. The first use lands, but by the later shame and closing beats the construction reads like a tic, flattening the prose into recognizable autopilot rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast at least two of the targeted turns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:161, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:239 so they land through concrete image, direct statement, or a different sentence shape. Keep at most the strongest one or two uses of the contrastive pattern.
findings.acceptance_test: After revision of the passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:161, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:239, no more than two instances of the "was not ... / it was ..." construction remain in chapter_05, and each revised beat still names the physical or emotional reality without abstract relabeling.

summary: Characterization and dialogue are the chapter's strongest elements: Celia is difficult, vain, funny, and observant; Elena reads as brisk rather than cruel; Marcia's public restraint and private arithmetic stay consistent with the style bible. The chapter still fails a default-critical review because the purse scene protects the central temptation with a moral safety valve, and the narration overuses explanatory class maxims and contrastive-definition sentence shapes, which keeps the prose below Booker-level control.
